entries.definition:
وَكَانَ الْإِنسَانُ أَكْثَرَ شَيْءٍ جَدَلًا but man is the most contentious of all creatures [lit. things].
جِدَال jidāl [v. n./n.] arguing/argument, disputing/disputation, contending/contention (2:197) فَلَا رَفَثَ وَلَا فُسُوقَ وَلَا جِدَالَ فِي الْحَجِّ there is no intercourse [also interpreted as: abusive speech], misbehaviour nor disputation during/on the pilgrimage.

* المُجَادَلَة ʾal-mujādalatu name of Sura 58, Medinan sura, so-named because of the reference in verse 1 to the ‘Dispute’ which took place between a woman and the Prophet.
entries.definition_english:
وَكَانَ الْإِنسَانُ أَكْثَرَ شَيْءٍ جَدَلًا but man is the most contentious of all creatures [lit. things].
جِدَال jidāl [v. n./n.] arguing/argument, disputing/disputation, contending/contention (2:197) فَلَا رَفَثَ وَلَا فُسُوقَ وَلَا جِدَالَ فِي الْحَجِّ there is no intercourse [also interpreted as: abusive speech], misbehaviour nor disputation during/on the pilgrimage.

* المُجَادَلَة ʾal-mujādalatu name of Sura 58, Medinan sura, so-named because of the reference in verse 1 to the ‘Dispute’ which took place between a woman and the Prophet.

entries.definition:
j–dh–dh to sever, to cut off, to break off; to hinder; fragments, breakage, shreds, chippings. Of this root, two words occur once each in the Qurʾan: مَجْذُوذ majdhūdh and جُذَاذ judhādh.
مَجْذُوذ majdhūdh [pass. part.] cut off, discontinued, interrupted, hindered (11:108) خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا مَا دَامَتِ السَّمَاوَاتُ وَالْأَرْضُ إِلَّا مَا شَاءَ رَبُّكَ عَطَاءً غَيْرَ مَجْذُوذٍ there to remain as long as the heavens and earth endure, unless your Lord wills otherwise–a gift unhindered.

جُذَاذ judhādh [coll. n./pl. of quasi pass. part. جَذِيذ jadhīdh] breakage, wreckage, pieces, fragments (21:58) فَجَعَلَهُمْ جُذَاذًا إِلَّا كَبِيرًا لَّهُمْ he broke them into pieces, except for a great one of them.
entries.definition_english:
j–dh–dh to sever, to cut off, to break off; to hinder; fragments, breakage, shreds, chippings. Of this root, two words occur once each in the Qurʾan: مَجْذُوذ majdhūdh and جُذَاذ judhādh.
مَجْذُوذ majdhūdh [pass. part.] cut off, discontinued, interrupted, hindered (11:108) خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا مَا دَامَتِ السَّمَاوَاتُ وَالْأَرْضُ إِلَّا مَا شَاءَ رَبُّكَ عَطَاءً غَيْرَ مَجْذُوذٍ there to remain as long as the heavens and earth endure, unless your Lord wills otherwise–a gift unhindered.

جُذَاذ judhādh [coll. n./pl. of quasi pass. part. جَذِيذ jadhīdh] breakage, wreckage, pieces, fragments (21:58) فَجَعَلَهُمْ جُذَاذًا إِلَّا كَبِيرًا لَّهُمْ he broke them into pieces, except for a great one of them.
